ddls:
CREATE TABLE "counties" (
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each county */,
  "county_name" TEXT /* Name of the county */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the county is located */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the county */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the county */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reported_cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reported case */,
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the county where the case was reported */,
  "cases_under_follow_up" INTEGER /* Number of cases currently under follow-up */,
  "newly_reported" INTEGER /* Number of newly reported cases */,
  "contacts_currently" INTEGER /* Number of contacts currently tracked */,
  "disease_type" TEXT /* Type of disease reported (e.g., COVID-19, Ebola) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the case was reported */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reported_cases_county_id FOREIGN KEY ("county_id") REFERENCES counties ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disease_types" (
  "disease_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease type */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the disease */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disease_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contacts" (
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact record */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the case with which the contact is associated */,
  "contacts_complete" INTEGER /* Number of contacts that have completed follow-up */,
  "contacts_lost_follow_up" INTEGER /* Number of contacts lost to follow-up */,
  "contacts_seen" INTEGER /* Number of contacts seen within the last 'd' days */,
  "contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the contact */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact */,
  "contact_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of contact (reference to contact_types) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contacts_contact_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("contact_type_id") REFERENCES contact_types ("contact_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contacts_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES reported_cases ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_types" (
  "contact_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact type */,
  "type_description" TEXT /* Description of the contact type (e.g., close contact, casual contact) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_follow_ups" (
  "follow_up_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each follow-up record */,
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the contact being followed up on */,
  "follow_up_date" TEXT /* Date of the follow-up action */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the follow-up (e.g., completed, pending) */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the follow-up (e.g., recovered, deceased) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the follow-up */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("follow_up_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contact_follow_ups_contact_id FOREIGN KEY ("contact_id") REFERENCES contacts ("contact_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contacts_removed" (
  "removed_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each removal record */,
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the contact that has been removed */,
  "removal_date" TEXT /* Date when the contact was removed from tracing */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for removal from tracing */,
  "removed_by" INTEGER /* User who removed the contact (reference to users) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("removed_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contacts_removed_removed_by FOREIGN KEY ("removed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contacts_removed_contact_id FOREIGN KEY ("contact_id") REFERENCES contacts ("contact_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "symptoms" (
  "symptom_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each symptom record */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the case associated with the symptom */,
  "symptom_description" TEXT /* Description of the symptom reported */,
  "date_reported" TEXT /* Date when the symptom was reported */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the symptom (e.g., mild, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("symptom_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_symptoms_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES reported_cases ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the user role (e.g., health official, epidemiologist) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role of the user (reference to user_roles) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the county for which analytics are recorded */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the analytics entry */,
  "total_cases" INTEGER /* Total number of cases reported */,
  "total_contacts" INTEGER /* Total number of contacts tracked */,
  "new_cases" INTEGER /* Number of new cases reported */,
  "new_contacts" INTEGER /* Number of new contacts tracked */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_county_id FOREIGN KEY ("county_id") REFERENCES counties ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who received the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting record */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the system setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the system setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
